name: Caramel Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Grease a 9x13 inch baking pan.
2. Sift the flour and then sift with 1 cup of packed brown sugar into a bowl.
3. In another bowl, combine butter or margarine, eggs, milk, salt, baking powder, and vanilla. Beat on medium high speed for 2 to 3 minutes using an electric mixer (use whisk attachment for best results). Spread the batter into the prepared pan.
4. Bake in preheated oven at 350 degrees F (175 degrees C) for 30 minutes or until a cake tester comes out clean. Remove from oven and let cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes before transferring to a serving dish. Once completely cooled, frost with caramel icing.
5. To Make Caramel Icing: In a saucepan over medium heat, stir together the remaining brown sugar, heavy cream until sugar is dissolved. Cover and cook for 3 minutes. Uncover and continue cooking without stirring until a candy thermometer reads 238 to 240 degrees F (113 to 115 degrees C). Stir in butter with a wooden spoon. Remove from heat and let cool to 110 degrees F (55 degrees C). Add remaining vanilla and beat until thick and creamy. If heavy, thin with cream until desired consistency is achieved. Spread over the cooled cake and top with chopped pecans.
